explanation: Steady air blows like from a compressed can of air.
Sharp air releases pressure releases a tennis call can popping open a fire extinguisher
Slow air releases a flat tire leak in an air pipe.
Air sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Air being sucked in a vacuum sucking in air the rush of air in a shopvac.
Aircraft doors some possible overlap with other DOOR categories or VEHICLES-DOOR.
For all manner of helicopters gyrocopters.
Interior recordings (mainly complex ambiences) of aircraft from cockpit interiors to passenger jet interiors.
All commercial and private jet powered aircraft military jets would go under AIRCRAFT-MILITARY.
Mechanical components of aircraft for example landing gear or levers and switches.
Military aircraft military fighter jets stealth bombers but any military aircraft goes here. Also military drones. Military helicopters could go here or in AIRCRAFT-HELICOPTER.
Aircraft not fitting another category in this list.
Aircraft using props as means of propulsion. A propeller airplane.
Toy hobby radio controlled UAV quadcopters RC jets and RC helicopters.
Jet powered rockets and rocket engines. missiles.
All alarms which use bells a school bell fire alarm bell a railroad crossing bell.
All alarms using buzzers or buzzers in general even if not technically an alarm would probably go here. A front door buzzer.
Specifically alarm clocks digital or analog. A bit of overlap with ALARMS-BELL but clock alarms of all kinds probably best to go here.
All electronic alarms pure electronic tone alarms. Home burglar alarms smoke detectors etc. Car alarms would go to VEHICLES-ALARMS.
Alarm sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Mechanical sirens like old klaxons. Vehicle sirens such a police fire and ambulance to go to VEHICLES-SIREN.
Quiet exterior 'air' with little activity like exterior room tones very simple exterior ambiences. The exterior equivalent of a room tone. If noticable activity find a better fitting category.
Mountain ambiences for recordings and ambiences geographically linked to Alpine Environments. Some overlap with AMBIENCE-RURAL.
Theme parks and carnivals. Beware of possible rights issues with uncleared music. Carnivals fairs festivals. Probably arcades too.
Group songbirds like a dawn chorus of pretty birds where the birds are the prominent sound. Single birds would go in BIRDS-SONGBIRD.
Parties New Years Eve. Not pure crowds as that would go in CROWDS but i.e. exterior city new years. Also see AMBIENCES-AMUSEMENT for something like a fair or festival.
Construction scenes with things like jackhammers cranes pile drivers composite construction scene road construction also.
Quiet desert ambiences with appropriate insects and birds. Maybe tumbleweeds.
Artificial designed ambience. V8.2 adds AMBIENCE-FANTASY and AMBIENCE-SCIFI which may be better fit for most 'designed' categories.
Crime scenes firefighting scenes accident scenes with things like firefighters police ambulances and medics. Also see VEHICLES-EMERGENCY.
Magical or other wordly composite ambiences soundscapes think Middle Earth or Pandora.
Specifically farm ambiences with animals maybe tractors working chickens etc. Isolated farm animals would go into ANIMALS-FARM.
Forest recordings or forest composite files. Trees and birds maybe wind in trees all together. Some overlap with AMBIENCE-RURAL or AMBIENCE-ALPINE.
Prairies meadows grazing patures etc. But FARMS has own category if farm animals or human activity are present.
Old West scenes Roman street scenes. Composite history and ancient ambiences.
Hi-tech machine rooms. Data centers control rooms. James Bond type lairs. Spaceship interiors.
Hospital ambiences of all kinds surgery and emergency rooms too. Possibly veterinarian hospitals too.
Industrial warehouses and factories plants or any ambience with an industrial feeling.
Thick ambiences of insects cicada and cricket beds mainly. May contain over sounds but main sound being large presence of insects. See also ANIMALS-INSECT for more isolated sounds.
Ambiences relating to lakes not isolated lapping but the lake version of AMBIENCE-SEASIDE. See also WATER-LAPPING.
Busy exterior or interior market activity and crowds. Think vibrant international markets in Istanbul or Africa but any market.
Ambience sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Relating to ships at sea shipyards things revolving around ships. (compare to AMBIENCE-SEASIDE). Water only recordings would go to a WATER category.
Workplaces offices people working typing telephones. General offices of various sizes.
Parks mostly city or suburban probably usually with some human activity city activity. Not pure nature sounds.
Prisons and jails reverberant inmate walla jail doors buzzers that sort of thing.
Protests Rallies Riots. Big mixed ambiences of crowds shouting yelling chanting. Opposed to AMBIENCE-CHEERING.
Except for restaurants for any other usually indoor public places such as stores lobbies hotels malls museums. Lots of possible overlap with other categories. Use if not fitting elsewhere.
Church and temples church services religious gatherings pilgrimages. Any ambience relating to religion.
Something busier than a room tone an Apartment a House with some activity from outside etc. Interior with exterior sounds coming through closed or open doors windows.
Restaurants and bars pubs places of eating and drinking mixed crowd with glass clinks or silverware.
Simple room tones with little or no activity. Also see now AMBIENCE-RESIDENTIAL if more than just air is present as it might be a better fit.
Rural and countryside ambiences that don't fit somewhere else in this list. Nature away from signs of people overlap with AMBIENCE-ALPINE DESERT FOREST PRAIRIE etc.
Schools of any age kindergarten through college hallways lockers voices school bells.
A composite sci-fi ambience a complex background layered with many sci-fi elements. A complex spacehip bridge or high-tech lab. See also SCIFI for individual elements.
Relating to beach and oceanside scenes with people. (Compare to AMBIENCE-NAUTICAL). Water only recordings would go to a WATER category.
Sporting events little league baseball to major league. Use CROWDS-SPORT for clean crowd recordings.
Suburban streets average residential neighborhood not dense like URBAN. Lawnmowers sprinklers birds.
A wetland often partially or intermittently covered with water; especially : one dominated by woody vegetation. Marshes although tecnically different are included here. Any wetland.
Something in between a CITY and a SUBURB. Activity cars pedestrians that 'small town' or village sound. May be confusion between this AMBIENCE-URBAN and AMBIENCE-SUBURBAN.
Specifically traffic without people. Not for a single car by but for clean busy traffic clean highway traffic for example. Rush hour where the sound is predominantly cars.
Not VEHICLES but public train stations airports bus stations subway stations. Not the airplane or train itself. But public places centering around transportation.
Jungles and rainforests lush animal life and vegetation.
Treeless environments where the ground is permanently frozen arctic antarctic.
Subterranean underground ambiences like sewers bunkers tunnels dungeons caves.
Underwater sounding ambiences deep bubbles and that classic undersea world sound. See also WATER-UNDERWATER.
Dense cities and environments. New York City or London or Mumbai. Often Dense Traffic horns pedestrians buses but really any city. Overlap with AMBIENCE-TOWN possible.
Battles warfare full ambiences here. Not for isolated WEAPONS or VEHICLES which have their own categories. Composite sounds of war.
Technically any amphibian but most likely used for frogs and toads.
All manner of marine and sea animals not just mammals but anything that lives in water.
Bat chirps echolocation and communicative vocalization. Wings would go under WINGS-MISC.
Domesticated house cats. See also ANIMALS-CAT WILD.
Wild cats as opposed to domesticated cats. Tigers and Lions.
All Domesticated dogs excluding wild dogs. Pet dogs. See also ANIMALS-WILD DOG for non-domesticated dogs.
Wild dogs and canines like Wolves Foxes Coyotes etc.
Domesticated mammals you'd find on a farm. For farm birds see BIRDS-FOWL. See also AMBIENCE-FARM.
All members of the horse family including donkeys and mules. See also FOOTSTEPS-HORSE and EQUIPMENT-BRIDLE.
All manner of traditional insect sounds crickets cicada flies and bees. And singular insect recordings. See also AMBIENCE-INSECT now for dense ambience style recordings.
Other animals not finding a clear category.
All members of the primate family except humans. Apes Chimps etc.
All reptiles including snakes turtles and lizzards.
All manner 'gnawing' animals like rats and mice. Rodents.
Any wild animal not explicitly defined by the list and not domesticated. Wolves and Coyotes now to go ANIMALS-DOG WILD
Re-recorded lines via the ADR process.
A delivered or stored asset most likely for video game use but something used in the development of a project.
Bounces made for picture department layered bounces.
Impulse Responses reverb and otherwise.
Loop group crowds or lines. Specifically recorded in a loop group envirment and needing to tracked as such.
Premixes final mixes mixdowns stems.
FX Stolen from production takes only. Beware of possible rights issues.
Production takes dialog lines and other spoken line production takes.
Raw recording files for example a load of a full DAT tape.
Reference material often restricted used for reference only. Beware of possible rights issues.
Pre-built scenes and soundscapes designed scenes. For example a fully build old-west scene.
Test tones used for calibration ADR beeps Pink Noise Sine wave Sweeps.
For trademarked or copyright sounds that you need to store in your library. Jingles logos.
Files that aren't fully metadata'd or mastered tag to remind you there is work to do. Recordings awaiting mastering.
Literally what it says 'What the F_ck' is THIS sound... Strange otherwise uncategorizable sounds.
Microwave oven beeps dishwasher beeps.
Beep sounds not fitting another category in this list. User Interface beeps go to UI-BEEPS.
Very low fidelity old 8-bit beeps.
Beeps from various medical devices EKG Life Support.
Specifically timer beeps counting down. Classic time bomb beeps.
Backup beeps from a large truck or construction vehicle.
Bells attached to animals like cow and sheep bells or a dog or cat collar with a bell on it.
Mechanical doorbells. Would also maybe electronic doorbells in here as well to avoid confusion probably. Also see ALARMS-BUZZERS for a door buzzer.
A large flat circular disc hit with a mallet. With v8.2 of the list most of these would now go into MUSICAL-BELLS instead.
Small to medium hand bells dinner bells. Musical bells like HANDBELLS and CARILLION now go into MUSICAL-BELLS.
Large bells like a church bell or tower bell or city hall bells Big Ben. Any large bell.
Bell sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Birds that prey on vertebrates especially rodents. Raptors.
Birds in Corvid family which includes crows and ravens.
Landfowl wildfowl and waterfowl also poultry. Ground feeding and many water feeding birds.
Bird sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Birds that live mostly near the ocean. Seagulls pelicans.
Specifically singing birds warblers robins thrushes what you imagine pretty dawn chorus birds.
Birds living in tropical locations jungle and tropical rainforest birds. Parrots and Toucans.
Long-legged long-billed birds that feed primarily in shallow water. Think Herons and Egrets.
Specifically large fan airboats used in swamps. Think the type of boat used in the Everglades. Also hovercraft.
The waves pushed up by a boat or ship going through the water a relatively clean recording.
Boat or Submarine doors. Some possible overlap with over DOOR categories or VEHICLES-DOOR.
Any boat powered by electricity. Fairly rare. Possible a a radio controlled boat or electric trolling motor.
Industrial fishing boats crab boats trawlers. Simple fishing boats to BOATS-MOTORBOAT.
Horns on boats and ships. Fog horns would go in HORNS-AIR category.
Interior ambiences on boats and ships ferries cruise ships navy ships.
All manner of boat mechanisms. Sailing ship rigging pulleys ropes ship wheels. See also BOATS-DOOR.
Specifically military boats and ships destroyers aircraft carriers. Any military boat.
Boat sounds not fitting another category in this list.
General small boats personal fishing boats speedboats pontoon boats. You average recreational boat.
Powered racing boats not for human powered racing boats those would go under BOATS-ROWING.
Canoes rowboats kayaks probably human powered boats that use oars and paddles even racing rowboats.
Composite recordings of rigging and sails isolated elements to CLOTH-FLAP for close sails or BOATS-MECHANISM for rigging.
Large ship passenger cruise ships oil tankers things relating to these.
Old steam paddle boats big steam ship sounds.
Relating to a submarine include sonar pings. Classic 'Dive' alarm would go in ALARMS-SIREN with keyword Klaxon.
Specifically boats and ships recorded underwater usually hydrophone recordings. SUBMARINES have their own subcategory.
Bullet pass bys in air or underwater.
Bullet hits and impacts on a bullet hit on any material from the ground to wood to a person.
Bullet sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Bullet ricochets that classic old west 'ping' when a bullet bounces off something.
Bullet shells ejecting and dropping to the ground. See also GUNS-HANDLING for things like loading or unloading a gun.
Cartoon animals made from animals calls props etc. Very silly or stylized animals calls for use in Cartoons.
Cartoon boings like for a bouncing cartoon character. Roger Rabbit or Tigger from Winnie the Pooh.
Cartoon clangs like a funny anvil hit. Most likely a very metallic resonant hit.
Cartoon creaks some overlap or confusion with CARTOON-STRETCH possible.
Cartoon horns as in bulb horns clown horns.
Cartoon hits bonk boink doink..
Cartoon machines an overly comical sounding machine or contraption. A Rube Goldberg contraption.
Cartoon sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Cartoon musical phrases. String plucks for a cartoon character sneaking up on another. Perhaps a cartoon musical phrase.
Cartoon string plucks violin plucks dental floss plucks.
Cartoon funny pops mouth pops suction cup pops.
Cartoon shake like a noisemaker toy maraca.
Cartoon splats funny liquid and mud like splats and things.
Cartoon squeaks. funny little sounding squeaks of things.
Cartoon stretches funny rubber or other type stretching sounds exaggerated.
Cartoon swishes specifically very funny cartoony swishes.
Cartoon twangs that classic ruler twang type sound.
Cartoon vehicles think Jetsons spaceship or Flintstones cars.
Cartoon vocals specifically very silly. See also CARTOON-ANIMALS for non-human vocals.
Cartoon warble.
Cartoon whistles most likely slide whistles other very silly whistles. A Wolf-Whistle.
Cartoon zips. Very fast 'zip' type sounds often for fast movement aways.
Ceramics break of a more singular nature a plate shatter breaking cracking crunching. Mostly for single items breaking. See also CERAMICS-CRASH & DEBRIS.
Ceramics destruction of more of a group nature multiple crashes and debris. See also CERAMICS-BREAK.
Ceramics friction scraping squeaking screeching.
Ceramics handling picking up a coffee mug handling a plant pot. Some overlap with CERAMICS-MOVEMENT.
Ceramics impacts without breaking so hits drops clunks and clinks. See also CERAMICS-BREAK.
Ceramics sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Ceramics single or group movement so dragging shaking and rattling. See also CERAMICS-HANDLE.
Ceramics ringing as in being bowed.
Chain snapping or breaking that sharp sound of a chain failing.
Chain handling repairing a bike chain a prisoner moving around in chains. Some overlap with CHAINS-MOVEMENT.
Chain impacts such a big chain hit or slam or a tension snap of a chain being pulled taut.
Chain sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Chain handling particularly being pulled through pulleys or across surfaces. See also CHAINS-HANDLE.
Chemical acid burning and sizzling bubbling. A vat of acid.
Chemical sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Chemical reactions chemistry set bubbling foaming bunsen burner reactions. Overlap perhaps with LIQUID & MUD category.
Chimes bells specifically for a clock Grandfather clock chimes. Alarm clocks would go to ALARMS-CLOCK.
Clocks winding complex mechanical clock mechanisms.
Clock sounds not fitting another category in this list. Also see ALARMS-CLOCK
Analog ticking clocks and watches of all sizes also mechanical stop watches.
Cloth flapping as in a flag flapping or superman's cape. Also ship sails and parachutes.
Cloth handling grabbing a jacket clothy arm grabs etc. Some overlap with CLOTH-MOVEMENT. See also possibly OBJECTS-FASHION.
Cloth impacts throwing a jacket forcefully to the floor a wet towel snap. Some overlap with CLOTH-MOVEMENT possible.
Cloth sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Varied cloth movement except flapping which has its own category. Variety of cloth movement. Cloth zuzz. See also CLOTH-FLAP.
Various cloth rips and tears short to long. Velcro would best fit here too.
Various AV devices such a projector or tape recorder video cameras here too. Some overlap or confusion with COMMUNICATIONS-CAMERA.
Still camera shutters automatic winding old flash-bulbs. Movie and video cameras to COMMUNICATIONS-AV.
Cellphones and Smartphones. clicks and rings tones.
Feedback and mic bumps and handling. Megaphones would go here too.
Communication sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Turntable Victrola motors and mechanics. STATIC has its own subcategory here.
Tuning radio and shortwave sounds. STATIC has its own subcategory here. A radio broadcast would go here too. Overlap with VOICES-FUTZED.
Static from any electronic source radio CB television optical noise phonograph noise.
Fax machine tones modem tones telegraph teletype. UI is it's own category
Old school rotary dial and digital telephones. Technically any telephone that isn't a mobile or cell phone.
Television programs on and off channel changes TV Remote controls. STATIC has its own subcategory here. A television broadcast would go here too. Overlap with VOICES-FUTZED.
Walkie Talkies CBs Ham Radios all two-way transceivers and radios go here. Put voices into VOICES-FUTZED.
Old school typewriters not modern computer keyboards.
Laptop or other hard drive seeking sounds data reading.
Typing on computer keyboard and mouse movement.
Computer sounds not fitting another category in this list. Printers would go to MACHINES-OFFICE.
Giant mutant squids or sea monsters kraken leviathans.
Alien or Fantasy birds or weird birdlike creatures. A giant mutant parakeet would go here.
Big four legged creatures and beasts. Wooly Mammoth a mutant elephant Mumakil.
Gelatinous and gooey type creatures gelatinous cube.
Dinosaurs of all types Jurassic Park.
Game of Thrones Lord of the Rings dragons of all types. See also CREATURES-REPTILIAN.
Creatures based on elements rock giants fire creatures ice monsters such a the Balrog or a Rock Giant.
Ghosts spirits ethereal vocals and wispy movement apparitions.
Human sized mythical creatures so zombies and vampires elves etc. Troll Ogres Orcs. Two legged creatures.
Giant spiders or other insect like creatures. The creature from The Fly. A giant Tarantula.
Creature sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Godzilla scary monsters horror monsters. See also CREATURES-HUMANOID.
Giant lizards and snakes large giant hissing monsters.
Specifically small mythical creatures. Fairies pixies small rodent like creatures etc. Distinguished by small size.
Source recordings meant for creature design like prop recordings human voice packs for design etc.
Crowds of upset or angry people as in a riot protest a schoolyard fight. See also AMBIENCE-PROTEST.
Crowds where the applause is predominantly featured. Use CROWDS-CHEERING for recordings that are mainly vocals.
Crowds in battle fighting yelling screaming dying. If including gunfire explosions. use AMBIENCE-WARFARE.
Crowds celebrating holidays parties rowdy crowds. If not clean use AMBIENCE-CELEBRATION.
Could include applause but cheering crowds where the crowd vocals are predominant. Overlap with CROWDS-CELEBRATION.
Relatively clean vocals of children together playing in a playground in a school classroom recess school hallway. See also AMBIENCE-SCHOOL.
A small enough group chat that you can discern words smaller and less active than a walla. A chatter.
Crowds laughing a comedy show canned television laughter groups of kids laughing at a party for example.
For loop group type walla usually performed for or to picture specific environments specific callouts. See also ARCHIVED-LOOP GROUP for official loop group recordings.
Crowd sounds not fitting another category in this list. See also MOVEMENT-CROWD.
Distressed crowds mixed screams and crying and reactions… crowds in a natural disaster or crowds in a giant monster attack...
Very quiet crowds mostly mumbling or murmuring. Like crowds whispering in church or a courtroom scene. See also VOICES-WHISPER for single person recordings.
Big Ooos and Aaahs and Booos big group responses to events happening. Shocked reactions.
Singing during sporting events New Year's Eve crowds singing Auld Lang Syne Christmas carols. Not large choral music which would go in MUSICAL category.
Clean crowds at sporting events Baseball football. For mixed ambience used AMBIENCE-SPORT. A sports chant.
Specifically very clean crowd walla with non-discernible words. See also various AMBIENCE categories.
Deep descending bass notes. Bass Drops.
Deep short and tight low end stingers big trailer deep hits. LFE type low end sweeteners.
Long deep horn like sounds. Like a War of the World's type long sound or like a long french horn braam type sound.
Particulary distored designed sounds and distortion elements.
Designed drones eerie tense drones long stylized sounds with not much activity or movement.
Often designed but tense strange undefined sounds that are eerie tense suspenseful. Some overlap with DESIGNED-DRONE possible.
Ethereal wispy designed sounds. Ghostlike whispers or heavenly tones. Otherworldly. See also DESIGNED-EERIE.
Granular synthesis sounds that have a gritty textural type sound think PaulStretch sounds broken down into tiny slices and recompiled.
Designed huge impacts collisions stylized big hits. Possible overlap with DESIGNED-STINGER.
Designed sounds not fitting another category in this list.
A stylized blending of elements something transitioning into another thing.
Tension risers things rising in tone something similar to a rising string line to create tension.
Designed rumbles for layering into a mix. Earthquake rumbles. Composite rumble.
Stattaco or glitchy machine type loops things with definied starts stops rhythmic stutters.
For recordings meant to be used as source for other designed sounds experimental recordings elements etc.
Stings stylized screen sweeteners. Big trailer type sounds. Big shock scare type sounds. Stylized stings hits and impacts. Overlap with DESIGNED-IMPACT.
Very synthesized sounds very electronic synth stuff. Raw work files of synth-play.
Designed sounds with a particularly tonal nature. The ringing in your ear of tinnitus for example.
Design specifically around vocals ghostly whispers enchanted spells reversed talking etc. Not for Creatures.
Stylized designed big whooshes definite possible overlap confusion with SHOOSHES-WHOOSH but this for less natural and more stylized versions.
Buildings and large mixed complex collapses a barn collapse Godzilla pushing over a building.
Mixed crashes complex crashes involving more than one material. So a combo huge glass and metal crash. See also individual material categories like METAL WOOD etc.
Destruction sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Dirt or sand settling as in after an explosion big sprays of dirt and sand. DUST has its own subcategory.
Settling dust dust clouds much lighter sound than DIRT & SAND-DEBRIS
Handling dirt & sand hands digging through dirt children making a sand castle. Some overlap with DIRT & SAND-MOVEMENT.
Big hits and impacts of dirt of sand dirt clods. Think the huge dirt ball impacts in WHERE THE WILD THINGS ARE.
Dirt Sand and Earth sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Pouring flowing of dirt or sand a big sand pour. See also DIRT & SAND-HANDLE.
Singing sand dunes. Probably pretty rare.
Very old sounding doors as in an old castle or abbey. But massive doors of this type probably to DOOR-DUNGEON.
Appliance doors such as oven dishwasher refrigerator or microwave.
Cabinet doors such as kitchen cabinet tool cabinets.
Fiberglass wood steel combination doors that don't sound like a specific material. A modern front door. Some confusion or overlap with DOORS-WOOD likely.
Door creaks wood or metal overlap with WOOD-FRICTION and METAL-FRICTION but specifically door creaks.
That big old classic wood and/or metal heavy dungeon castle type door.
Electric doors of all kinds garage doors big soundstage doors sliding store doors. See also DOORS-REVOLVING.
Non-motorized gates metal or wood. So a garden gate a park gate. A farm gate.
Glass doors manual shopping store doors glass home front doors. Most store doors would probably fit here.
Door knobs door locks deadbolts chain locks push bars. Lock Picking Doorknob Jiggles.
James bond type doors something not quite sci-fi but more advanced Modern lab or air sealed chamber vaults. See also SCIFI-DOOR.
Self closing doors lots of office and store doors are of this type. Also automatic doors if hydraulic or pneumatic.
Knocking on various door surfaces.
Metal doors like a vault door or heavy door. PRISON doors have their own category as they are quite specific sounding.
Door sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Doors made out of plastic. Not very common but maybe a door to a child's play house.
Big metal jail and prison doors. Sliding prison doors or swinging jail doors.
Classic revolving doors used as entries to hotels banks malls.
Manual sliding doors such as a patio door sliding shower doors. Electric sliding doors to DOORS-ELECTRIC.
Mostly a fantasy type door but think Gates of Moria big tombstone vault doors.
Swinging doors like old-west saloon or church pulpit doors. Doors on swinging springs.
Wood doors home school most general door category probably.
Metal drawers like on a toolbox.
Drawer sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Plastic drawers maybe on a parts bin.
Kitchen drawers bathroom drawers. Most drawers would probably fall into this category.
A longer movement of electricity think Jacob's ladder or a power station switching arc when decoupled.
Ground hum buzzes steady electrical hums guitar pickup hum amp hum. Any steady electric hum or buzz.
Electromagnetic field recordings specifically.
Electric sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Think sparklers or a downed power line emitting sparks welding... A singular spark would probably be a ZAP.
A short shocking fast percussive electrical sound. Or a repetition of short fast sounds. A taser is a series of ZAPS.
Equipment for animals use. Horse bridle reigns leashes harnesses dog leashes saddle straps.
Hi-Tech gear things like spacesuits complex suits with lots of gizmos or complex parts.
Equipment sounds not fitting another category in this list. See also TOOLS and OBJECT categories.
Recreational gear like backpacks hiking gear rock climbing harnesses camping tents. Specifically outdoor recreation. See also OBJECTS-GYM.
Gear worn while playing sports American football shoulder pads hockey gear etc.
Military and police belts bandoliers ammo belts. Military gear troop gear.
Designed or stylized explosions and sweeteners. Hyperreal explosions.
Explosion sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Real explosion recordings realistic explosions not overly stylized. (FIREWORKS has its own category)
Fake or designed farts whoopie cushion flarp mouth farts.
Fart sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Real recordings of farts.
Bodyfalls but probably also body scuffles on ground from simple to complex. On any surface.
Active or big scuffling cloth big fight cloth movement.
Big arm grabs hand or arm blocks as well as they're similar sounds grappling during wrestling or martial arts.
Fight impacts blocks kicks and punches. BODYFALLS has its own subcategory here as do GRABS.
Fight sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Covers normal fire burning mostly roars not isolate fire crackle which have their own category. See also new FIRE-TURBULENT category.
A sharp and loud release of fire covers fire bursts big flare ups. Think a backdraft explosion of fire. A flamethrower. Usually a single event.
Specifically crackly fire elements isolated crackle or a very crackly fire good for layering to other fire.
Gas burners as you'd find in a kitchen stove or gas grill. That sort of hissy or airy fire burn. Crematoriums and furnaces.
Fire ignites including matches lighters. That initial spark of ignition of a fire.
Fire sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Glowing red pokers fuses coal embers when extinguished sauna rocks when water is poured on them. Glowing red hot.
Constant torches and torch movement that phasey fire sound as a torch is moved around.
Wildly whooshing turbulent violent fire. Larger than just burning think fire tornado. Extreme moving fire.
Big whooshy fire events like a fireball through the air.
Large commercial fireworks mortars as on the 4th of July. Full displays or single large mortars.
Firework sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Small store-bought recreational fireworks firecrackers and roman candles and bottle rockets.
Used for 'performed' official and very clean Foley done to picture. For your own wild recordings use FOOTSTEPS and OBJECT categories.
Used for 'performed' official and very clean Foley done to picture. For your own wild recordings use FOOTSTEPS and OBJECT categories.
Used for 'performed' official and very clean Foley done to picture. For your own wild recordings use FOOTSTEPS and OBJECT categories.
Used for 'performed' official and very clean Foley done to picture. For your own wild recordings use FOOTSTEPS and OBJECT categories.
Used for 'performed' official and very clean Foley done to picture. For your own wild recordings use FOOTSTEPS and OBJECT categories.
Things overtly related to cooking baking preparation chopping.
Specifically drinking would also cover animals drinking dogs lapping water a horse drinking.
Specifically eating would also cover animals eating a dog eating or a horse eating. Swallowing chewing.
Glass and bottle movement specifically at a restaurant or bar. Isolated glassware recordings.
Nuts seeds grains things like that. Raw food recordings often used for other design purposes vegetable recordings. See also GORE-SOURCE.
Pots and pans movement big metal bowls prep bowls.
Food and Drink sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Pouring liquid water beer wine champagne etc. Some overlap with WATER-POUR but specifically consumption liquids.
Silverware and/or plate movement in a restaurant or dinner at home. GLASSWARE it's own subcategory.
Individual animal footsteps dog elephant. Group animals like a herd or stampede to MOVEMENT-ANIMALS. See also FOOTSTEPS-HORSE
Monster feet Put dinosaur feet in here too but also for small fantasy creatures.
All manner of horse feet from single to running.
Human feet any surface barefoot shoes it all goes here.
Footstep sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Not for arcade ambience which would go under AMBIENCE-AMUSMENT but for specific Pinball machines upright video games games of skill like Skeeball.
Board games with pieces think Monopoly Risk chess checkers.
Casino slot machines Keno machines roulette craps.
Game sounds not fitting another category in this list. Cards would go here. Jenga.
Console video games from Atari to PS5 designed fake video games for consoles like Playstation.
Geothermal steam vent. A hole crack or fizzure releasing gases.
Geysers and eruptions as you'd find in Yellowstone.
Lava flows crackles could be some overlap with FIRE but overtly lava sounding things go here.
Geothermal sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Mudpots and paint pots popping liquidy mud sounds.
Glass singular breaks so a bottle smash or a window shatter.
Glass crashes and debris so multiple items crashing and isolated glass shards and debris.
Glass friction such as a scrape or squeak. Could be some overlap with GLASS-TONAL.
Glass objects being handled grabbing a bottle or glass. Some overlap with GLASS-MOVEMENT.
Glass impacts without breaking so a bottle hit that doesn't break a bottle dropping to the ground but not breaking.
Glass sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Glass movement such as a case of bottles rattling or a bottle rolling on the floor. See also GLASS-HANDLE.
Glass tonal sounds like bowed blass a glass harmonica or a bowed crystal bowl.
Gore blood sounds dripping and flowing. Likely some overlap with LIQUID & MUD but this for particularly 'gory' versions.
Gore bones bones crunching snapping break. Put creature JAW SNAPS here too.
Gore flesh sizzling possible overlap with FIRE-SIZZLING but this more exaggerated for flesh burning.
Gore flesh tearing ripping peeling. Very graphic flesh sounds. A monster ripping someone apart.
Gore sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Gore gross oozing liquid open wounds possible overlap with LIQUID categories but this hyperreal and over the top.
Recordings meant as source for GORE sounds smashing a watermelon stabbing a pumpkin etc.
Gore splats gross splats of gooey stuff. A body or head exploding and hitting the wall. Hyperreal.
Gore squishes disgusting squishes or flesh and organs. A dinosaur stepping on a person and squishing them.
Gore sharp stabs and sword pulls. That classic sound of someone being impaled. More crunchy than a SQUISH.
Old black powder weapons muskets.
Modern big guns guns that launch shells. Not CANNONS which have their own group.
Fully automatic capable weapons even when firing singly. Machine guns.
Pirate and Civil War cannons for modern artillery see GUNS-ARTILLERY.
Alongside GUN-MECHANISM but specifically gun handling grabs holstering dropping catching.
Not quite a sci-fi Laser Gun but something more advanced than a normal gun a rail gun for example.
Gun various mechanical sounds clips cocking bolt slides.
Gun sounds not fitting another category in this list. BULLETS has its own category.
Handguns and pistols.
Rifles single shot and semi-automatic.
Shotguns single dual pump.
Silenced Weapons silencers.
Air horns as in handheld compressed air horns huge factory air horns. A fog horn. BOATS-HORN has it's own category for ship and ferry horns as does TRAINS-HORN.
Party horns from small party horns to vuvuzelas.
Horn sounds not fitting another category in this list for example hunting calls. Not brass musical instruments which has it's own category in MUSICAL.
Rams horns conches alpine horns old traditional horns medieval battle horns.
Blowing air blowing up a balloon blowing out candles etc. See also HUMAN-BREATH for less forceful breathing.
Breathing normal to heavy big inhales sighs.
Human belches burps.
Human coughs and coughing.
Heartbeats both literal as in a Dr. office and stylized or designed heartbeats.
Human kissing.
Human sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Human urinating.
Human skin Scratching handshakes skin on skin action back rubbing etc.
Human sneezing.
Human sniffing smelling.
Human snoring.
Human spits.
Human vomiting.
Ice cracks breaks shatters. Any ice breaking that is fairly singular or isolated large or small.
Ice large crashes and isolated debris go here. Iceberg calving.
Dry ice may go here but often it's a metallic sound too but ice scrapes squeaks creaks.
Ice handling picking up ice. Some overlap with ICE-MOVEMENT possible.
Pieces of ice dropping (without breaking). Ice hits solid ice impacts that don't break.
Ice sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Ice sliding or rattling maybe an object sliding across ice. See also ICE-HANDLE.
Tonal ice sounds probably fairly rare but that ice ping on a frozen lake would be one example.
Steady laser beams not a laser gun. So a James Bond style laser a scientific laser.
Classic laser guns blasters laser cannons. That thing that Ben Burtt made famous.
Classic laser impact sounds from blaster hits.
Laser sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Leather creaks jacket and bag creaks saddle movements. Any creaking leather.
Leather handling picking up a leather bag putting on a leather jacket maybe. Some overlap with LEATHER-MOVEMENT possible.
Hits and impacts of leather leather bag or saddle dropping.
Leather sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Movement of Leather definitely overlap with LEATHER-CREAK and LEATHER-HANDLE.
Mud and thicker than water thick bubbles viscous liquid. Things like Oil gloopy thick liquids.
Mud and thicker than water Splats and hit. Things like Oil gloopy thick liquids.
Mud and thicker than water sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Mud and thicker than water oozing slime glooping. Things like Oil gloopy thick liquids.
Thicker than water disgusting liquidy suction plunger sucks. Things like Oil gloopy thick liquids.
Theme park rides such as rollercoasters and carnival rides. Carnival game machines. General ambiences to go in AMBIENCE-AMUSEMENT.
Old mechanical sewing machines ancient looms olive presses a bit ambiguous but a very old mechanical machine.
General appliances refrigerator stove oven vacuum cleaner... AC and fans have their own category.
A machine for construction that isn't a vehicle or a tool. For example a cement mixer a woodchipper.
Elevator movement and doors would go here.
Escalators and moving walkways would go here.
Fans of all types box fans ceiling fans. For air conditioning use MACHINES-HVAC.
Powered landscaping machines lawnmowers weed-whackers edgers hedge trimmers. See also TOOLS-GARDEN and OBJECTS-GARDEN.
Exercise bikes weight machines treadmill all things fitness. See also OBJECTS-GYM
Think cool James Bond machines big elaborate death traps maybe Rube Goldberg type machines. See also SCIFI-MACHINE.
Furnaces Air Conditioners. Fans go in MACHINES-FAN. A close up gas furnace flame would go to FIRE-GAS.
Big industrial presses factory machines industrial robots any heavy manufacturing machines.
Complex mechanical smaller machines and gadgets puzzle boxes that open portals to hell. Usually smaller devices.
Machines typically found in a hospital medical lab or Doctor's office.
Machine sounds not fitting another category in this list. See also MOTOR category.
Machines typically found in an office such as printers and copy machines.
Air or water pumps fish tank aquarium pumps sump pumps. Air compressors would fall into this category.
Angelic Holy Benevolent magic. The opposite of evil. Think choral tones and healing magic.
Magic centered around elements fire water ice. Think the movie Frozen type stuff.
Evil magic ominous dark magic. Voldemort type stuff.
Magic sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Shorter magic phrases often for transforming short 'turn you into a rabbit' type stuff.
Glassy or chime-like shimmers waves of particle type magic.
Something complex for a spell being cast. Think Maleficent swirling green magic or weird vocal-ish spells.
Short clicks could be from button presses things like that. Some confusion and overlap with MECHANICAL-SWITCH.
A toothed cog turning or grinding gears complex gear mechanisms. Small clock gears would go in CLOCKS-MECHANICS.
Various hydraulic or pneumatic sounds like on a self-closing door. Isolated hydraulics and pneumatics. See also DOORS-HYDRAULIC & PNEUMATIC for a complete door.
Latch as in a briefcase latch or a luggage latch.
A sliding or switching lever. Train track lever lever to turn on an electric chair. Old Frankenstein machine levers breaker levers.
A padlock bicycle lock or a locking mechanism. Door locks to go into DOORS-HARDWARE.
Mechanical sounds not fitting another category in this list.
An isolated recording of a pulley or system of pulleys.
That sound a ratchet makes.
Old train station counting board flipping. A old communications relay board.
Like a rolling conveyor belt or something on a roller.
Basic and heavy switches light switches power switches. Some overlap or confusion with MECHANICAL-CLICK.
Metal breaking snaps cracks rips shatters.
Metal crashing and debris. Complex metal crashes group metal crashes metal debris falls.
Metal friction stress so scrapes creaks squeaks squeals screeches. Metal sounds caused by friction.
Metal handling handling metal objects. Some overlap with METAL-MOVEMENT possible.
Metal single item impacts so anvil hits dropping something that doesn't break. Clangs clunks bangs.
Metal sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Metal movement so drag roll rattle shake. Single or metal items moving. Or movement on a metal surface. See also METAL-HANDLE.
Metal tonal resonating ringing wronks and warps. Bending sheet metal a singing bowl ringing metal shinging.
Old motors usually combustion so old oil pump engines old compressors. An antique gas pump for example.
Non-vehicle combustion engines. Combustion motors generators. Isolated engine recordings.
Non-vehicle electric motors. Any motor that runs on electricity. An isolated electric motor. See also TOOLS-POWER.
Motor sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Specifically servo motors from robot to radio controlled servos. Clean isolated servos.
Turbines water or air. That classic 'whine' power up and down are usually turbines. A jet engine is a turbine.
Crowds moving but also performing activities cleaning moving boxes. Clean recordings with no vocals. See MOVEMENT-HUMAN for Single activity.
Group animal movement individual feet go to FOOTSTEPS-ANIMAL or FOOTSTEPS-HORSE. But think herds of grazing animals stampedes.
Slithering Dragons Gooey Zombie Movement.
Groups of people milling or walking. Usually mostly just recordings of group footsteps in real locations. No vocals. Compare to MOVEMENT-ACTIVITY.
Movement of single human aside from footsteps standing sitting shuffe. Some overlap or confusion with FOOTSTEP-HUMAN or MOVEMENT-ACTIVITY.
Group insects footsteps movement swarms spiders locusts. Swarms of flying insects here too bees flies. Overlap with ANIMALS-INSECT or AMBIENCE-INSECT.
Movement sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Very light movement people in church adjusting people on church pews. Again recordings without vocals. Compare to MOVEMENT-ACTIVITY and MOVEMENT-CROWD.
Specifically musical bells tuned bells think Tubular bells or hand bells. See also the BELL category for non-musical uses. BELL-GONG is older and this is a better fit.
Brass musical instruments trumpets tuba french horn.
Wind chimes bell trees Tinkerbell type magic chimes.
Choir pieces choral not for 'designed type vocally stuff'.
Strange or wierdly modified instruments exotic instruments prepared instruments like a prepared piano.
Various instruments that don't have a home in the other MUSICAL categories.
Keyed Instruments such as pianos and harpsichord. For synthesizer though see MUSICAL-SYNTHESIZED.
For looping musical phrases sampled loops.
Music sounds not fitting another category in this list. Hopefully rarely needed.
Drums and percussion including deep timpani or big war drums. Mainly meant for true membrane instruments also see MUSICAL-TUNED PERCUSSION
Tuned percussion mostly played with mallets Xylophones Vibraphones etc.
A musical performance or warm-up a live musical act a street musican high school band. If a mixed ambience look to AMBIENCE categories like PUBLIC PLACE.
Guitars Banjos Harps instruments that are mainly plucked when played. See also MUSICAL-STRINGED.
Musical samples. Single triggered samples not loops which has its own subcategory here.
Shaken musical instruments rattles tamourines.
Complete musical pieces songs as ringtones car music bys. See also MUSICAL-PERFORMANCE which is a better fit for 'live performances'.
A short musical phrase or accent like a horror 'string' sting. A big pitching cello note. But musical in nature.
Bowed string instruments like violin cello viola. Harp probably now goes to MUSICAL-PLUCKED.
Synth samples musical synth performances. Some overlap with MUSICAL-SAMPLE but this meant for more than simple triggered samples.
Toys that play musical phrases also music boxes would go here.
Woodwind instruments like flute oboe bassoon clarinet.
Composite big movements of sliding falling earth landslides mudslides. This category often for big hollywood versions of these types of sound.
Composite and complex earthquake rumbles and rattles. This category often for big hollywood versions of these types of sound.
Natural Disaster sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Composite tornado sounds layered and created massive tornadoes. This category often for big hollywood versions of these types of sound.
Composite tidal wave or tsunami scenes layered and created. This category often for big hollywood versions of these types of sound.
Composite typhoons cyclones or hurricanes. Massive swirling storms. This category often for big hollywood versions of these types of sound.
Composite big volcano eruptions. Lava lava bombs eruptions. This category often for big hollywood versions of these types of sound. See also GEOTHERMAL-LAVA.
Bags and purses shopping bags lunch bags. Really any bag except Luggage which would go to OBJECTS-LUGGAGE.
Books magazines sounds relating to these. Handling particularly. Some overlap or confusion with PAPER-HANDLE.
Coins land toss drop handle. Coins being counted sorted. A bag of coins.
Boxes tupperware jewelry boxes things that hold things lids handling opens and closes.
Shoes belts clothes actions involding fashion objects. Makeup lipstick etc. Some overlap possible with CLOTH categories. See also OBJECTS-JEWELRY. And OBJECTS-BAG.
Chairs movement couch movement things like that. Wood chair creaks could go in WOOD-FRICTION.
Flower pots bird feeders things that you might find and use in a garden. See also TOOLS-GARDEN and MACHINES-GARDEN.
Objects found in a fitness center or school gym maybe a yoga mat dumbells. See also MACHINES-GYM.
Cleaning products hangers towel racks things around the house that don't fit somewhere else.
Bracelets necklaces rings.
Keys jingling keys movement in lock.
Suitcases luggage related sounds handling opening packing.
Common objects in hospital or medical office.
Object sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Things found in an office staplers paper cutter etc. Not machines like a printer which would go in MACHINES-OFFICE.
Items commonly used in packaging or packing so bubblewrap styrofoam labels food packaging.
Tape and other tacky sticky type sounds as well. Velcro probably better into CLOTH-RIP as that matches the 'sound' better.
Umbrella opens and closes. Not for rain on umbrella which would go in RAIN-CLOTH.
Non-motorized wheeled carts and dollies. Shopping cart food cart baggage cart baby stroller dolly wheelchairs.
Any writing object or performance of writing on any surface. Pens pencils crayons markers.
Pant zippers bag zippers luggage zippers.
Paper and Cardboard. Tossing a group of pages or a book in the air money flutters as it falls to the ground. Confetti.
Paper and Cardboard. Sliding paper or scraping paper.
Paper and Cardboard. Page turns magazine handling. Books dropping. Handling paper money. Some overlap or confusion with OBJECTS-BOOK.
Paper and Cardboard. Heavy paper hits a rolled up newspaper thwack a magazine hitting something.
Paper and Cardboard. Paper sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Paper and Cardboard. Ripping paper paper tears.
Paper and Cardboard. Not very common but tonal paper sounds.
Plastic breaking crunches rips shatters.
Plastic crashing groups items falling group destruction plastic debris.
Plastic friction such as scrapes creaks squeaks screeching.
Plastic handling handling a toy or remote control. Some overlap with PLASTIC-MOVEMENT possible.
Plastic item impact. Hit drop big plastic impacts where item does not break. Dropping a plastic object.
Plastic sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Plastic movement dragging plastic or drag on plastic rattles shakes. See also PLASTIC-HANDLE.
Plastic tonal sounds with a musical quality. Probably pretty rare.
Rain on cloth like a person's clothes or more likely a patio umbrella awning.
Rain on asphalt or concrete or patio tile. Rain on a hard slappy solid surface.
General downpour rain on mixed materials.
Rain on a glass roof like a sunroof or rain blown against a window as well.
Interior recordings of Rain from inside house or barn or vehicle.
Rain on a metal surface so exterior rain on a car or metal sheet metal roof.
Rain on plastic or vinyl surfaces like a grill cover or a plastic shed.
Rain on plants leaves and grass. Jungle or tropical rain on plant leaves.
Rain on water puddles lakes ponds.
Rain on wood surfaces so a deck for example.
Robot sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Complex robot movement servos possible robot hovering and flying. See also MOTORS-SERVO.
Droid and robot vocals R2D2 would go here. Any manner of vocalizing robot. More expressive than a beep/UI. Robot language.
Rock cracks breaks rips or shatters. More isolated and singular cracks and breaks.
Rock crashes and debris so a huge rock crash collapse. Or isolated debris. See also NATURAL DISASTER-AVALANCHE.
Rock creaks scrapes... the stress of rock about to break.
Rock handing picking up or digging through rocks bricks. Some overlap with ROCKS-MOVEMENT possible.
Rock falling dropping without breaking or something hit with a rock a big rock impact.
Rock sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Rock dragging or dragging across a rock surface rocks shaking or rattling. See also ROCKS-HANDLE.
Tonal rocks probably pretty rare. Singing rocks.
Rope stretches and creaks people hanging from nooses.. Ship rope creaks. Tension of rope stretches.
Rope handling tying knots rigging up a ship sail. Possible overlap with ROPE-MOVEMENT.
Rope impacts tossing ropes to ground ropes banging violently in the wind. Possible overlap with ROPE-MOVEMENT.
Rope sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Ropes zuzzing moving across objects zipline. See also ROPE-HANDLE.
Rubber items crashing or rubber debris landing.
Rubber squeaks balloon squeaks a rubber shoe squeak.
Rubber handling rubber objects being manipulated or handled. Some overlap with RUBBER-MOVEMENT
Rubber impacts drops bounces. Hitting a balloon or rubber ball.
Rubber sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Rubber movement dragging rubber or drag on rubber. Rubber flapping. See also RUBBER-HANDLE.
Rubber movement with a tonal quality a musical quality.
Overly futuristic alarms the kind you're hear on a spaceship
Use Sci-Fi for overtly 'science fiction' type computer sounds a very futuristic computer. Some overlap with USER INTERFACE.
That Star-Trek type door. Spaceship doors.
Energy particles vortexes transporters holograms force fields.
A bolt of SciFi energy a hi-tech energy impact more futuristic than just an explosion. Overlap with DESIGNED-IMPACT.
Future technology machines transporters food replicators.
An overly sci-fi sounding gizmo or mechanism. Little unfolding robot or something like that. Similar to MACHINE-MECHANISM but more SciFi.
SCI-FI sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Cheesy sci-fi sounds like from The Jetsons The Day the Earth Stood Still. Old TV or film sci-fi sounds.
Spaceships here your best Enterprise and Tie Fighter rip-offs! See also SCIFI-VEHICLE for non-spaceships.
Various sci-fi vehicles like a moon rover landspeeder speeder bike hoverboard podracer. See also SCIFI-SPACESHIP.
Lightsabers exotic sci-fi weapons. Not a 'blaster' which would go in LASERS-GUN.
Snow big crashes a big fall of snow a roof full of snow collapsing. See also NATURAL DISASTER-AVALANCE for the extreme version of this.
Snow scrapes squeaks. Skiing on snow sledding.
Snow being handled a snowball being formed etc. Digging out of show with your hands. Some overlap with SNOW-MOVEMENT.
A big impact of snow like a roof full of snow falling. Or smaller like a snowball. Jumping into a pile of snow.
Snow sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Dragging something through snow rolling in snow. See also SNOW-HANDLE.
Sports played on a court indoors or out such a basketball tennis.
Sports played on a field outdoors such as soccer and football.
Sports played or performed primarily in a gym and/or ring such as boxing wrestling gymnastics. See also SPORTS-INDOOR.
Various indoor sports not able to be categorized elsewhere.
Sport sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Wheeled skating sports like skateboarding or roller skating rollerblading.
Track and field sports as in running long jump pole vault. Think common summer Olympic games.
Sports played in or on water swimming diving. Aquatic sports.
Sports on snow or ice such as hockey ice skating snowboard and skiing.
A swish is smaller than a Whoosh. Arm Swishes Sword Swishes. Small objects make Swishes.
A whoosh is larger than a swish. A large object would make a Whoosh. Tanks flying over your head or an asteroid by. See also DESIGNED-WHOOSH for very stylized whooshes.
Hand tools used in a garden rakes hoes etc. Also see MACHINES-GARDEN for powered machines. See also OBJECTS-GARDEN for non-tools.
Hand tools not requiring power. Hammers wrenches pliers. See also TOOLS-GARDEN.
Tool sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Air tools such as jackhammers air ratchets nail guns. The air-wrench sound in a tire shop.
Tools that require power. A sander electric drill dremel tool circular saw.
Remote controlled car slot cars. Any kids toy that required power. See also AIRCRAFT-REMOTE CONTROLLED.
Wind up robots things like that. Toys with some mechanism or mechanical component.
Toy sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Screeching metalic train brake wheels subway brakes. Usually a piercing sound.
Train clacks isolated constant clacking of trains passing or running.
Train doors sliding doors in passenger or freight trains. Some overlap with general DOORS category and possibly VEHICLES-DOOR.
Freight trains large commercial diesel locomotives.
Commuter trains elevated trains light rail SUBWAYS have their own category.
Hi-Tech trains Maglev monorails Hyperloops bullet trains.
Train horns subway horns. A steam train whistle would likely go into WHISTLES-MECHANICAL.
Interior trains from Diesel to Commuter onboard train sounds.
Coupling decoupling and other mechanical sounds relating to trains. Levers coal tender mechanics that are specific to a train.
Train sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Old steam powered trains big smoke stack chugs.
Subways overtly 'underground' sounding trains above group city trains probably best in TRAINS-ELECTRIC
Trolleys cable cars trams and streetcars. A Tram is 'pulled' along a cable so not self-powered. An electric tram would go to TRAINS-ELECTRIC.
An alert tone or chime or notification. Something that gets Attention of User startup chimes.
Interface beeps beeps while interacting with various technology phones computers. Also see BEEPS category.
Typing clicks button press clicks response to input.
User interface calculations scanning thinking text displayed on screen. Screen gak or garble.
On Screen Glitches or Static. Malfunctioning computer or OS.
User Interface sounds not fitting another category in this list.
User interface slides swipes scrolls. Open Close Appear DIsappear.
Individual movement of grass elements in a studio or exterior environment.
Individual movement of leaf elements in a studio or exterior environment. Jumping into a pile of leaves.
Vegetation and Foliage sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Individual movement of tree and branch elements in a studio or exterior environment.
Not sirens but just Car alarms car alarm arm and disarm chirps.
Very old vehicles such a Model A Model T. Mainly cars but possible other very old vehicles trucks tractors.
All-Terrain Vehicle three and four-wheelers dune buggies side by side.
Bicycles recumbent bikes unicycles too. Any manner of pedal powered bicycle.
Brake squeals and screeches of all kinds. TRAINS-BRAKE has its own category.
City buses school buses Greyhound buses.
All cars except overtly antique... add ERA to metadata to make searches easier. 1950s 1960s...
Cranes bulldozers paving vehicles any construction related vehicle goes here.
Car van truck. Their doors all go here.
Electric golf carts electric scooters electric cars. A Tesla. Any variety of electric vehicle even electric motorcycles.
Specifically emegency vehicles like Firetrucks Ambulances Police cars. Isolated sirens would go still to VEHICLES-SIREN.
I'd put farm tractors here Some overlap possibly with CONSTRUCTION vehicles. A riding lawn mower would best to MACHINES-GARDEN maybe.
Big Rigs Lorry Semi-truck U-Haul trucks not for TRAINS which have their own category.
Generic or general vehicle pass bys not 'traffic'. Bys for a specific car go with that car. For group passbys use AMBIENCE-TRAFFIC.
Specifically car and vehicles horns. BOATS-HORNS and TRAINS-HORN have their own category. Fog horn would go into HORNS-AIR.
Interior recordings (ambiences) for car truck van interiors from passenger cars to back of a moving truck riding inside a bus.
Backfiring poorly running vehicle. False starts sputtering... In foreign language translate to MALFUNCTIONING. See also CARTOON-VEHICLE.
Various mechanisms on a vehicle like seatbelts window cranks door locks.
Overtly military ground vehicles. Tanks and military trucks for example. Armored Personnel Carriers military Jeeps. See also AIRCRAFT-MILITARY and BOATS-MILITARY.
Vehicles sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Scooters Motorcycles Vespas Motocross… All things 2 wheeled and powered. ATV goes to VEHICLES-ATV.
Racing cars or drag racing cars. Indy and Formula 1 cars monster trucks BOATS-RACING has its own category.
Police Firetruck Ambulance sirens. Any vehicle siren. See also VEHICLES-EMERGENCY if engine sounds are present also.
Tire skids peel-outs. Other tire sounds go in VEHICLES-TIRES.
Suspension squeaks and rattles for any car truck semi. Suspensions springs. A car or truck on a very rough road.
Tire rolls on various surfaces tire whines no engine sounds isolated tire sounds. A flat tire flop.
Passenger Trucks pickup trucks Vans and SUVs. Commercial trucks go in VEHICLES-FREIGHT.
Golf carts cushmans traffic gator small motorized utility vehicles.
Old wood wagon horse drawn carts carriages buggies. A Stagecoach.
Powered windows for any vehicles crank windows should go in VEHICLES-MECHANISM.
Weird alien vocals here.
Very young babies through toddler non-word vocals cut vocals.
A single person cheering. For a group use CROWDS-CHEERING.
Various vocals for a child or a teenager. For non-discernible vocals for a very young child use VOICES-BABY.
A single person crying.
A single fight and vocal efforts single grunts and things like that. Boxing match vocals tennis grunts.
Any generic female vocal or dialog or misc vocal that doesn't fit somewhere else.
P.A. Speakers Airport announcements Hospital announcements.
For storing famous speeches famous news broadcasts presidential speeches.
A single person laughing.
Any generic male vocal or dialog or misc vocal that doesn't fit somewhere else.
The Voice category is for single voices. Groups of people go into the CROWDS categories.
A single Ooo or Aah and things of that nature.
A single scream or yell battle cry agonizing death.
A single voice singing a child singing. For a crowd use CROWDS-SINGING
A single whispering voice. The crowd equivalent would be CROWDS-QUIET.
Single or continuous bubbles. Water boiling on a stove fish tank bubbles.
Draining gurgling water pulling in air with it as it drains think a kitchen sink drain or a storm drain shower drain.
Water dripping slow to fast. A cave drip a leaking pipe dripping or a kitchen faucet dripping.
That sound of Alka-Seltzer in a cup air fizzing in water. Carbonated beverages.
General water flowing in our case as in a river creek or stream. Steady water. Rapids would fall under WATER-TURBULENCE and WATERFALL has its own subcategory
Water features and fountains trickles or flows for a fountain splashy fountains. From tiny to large city fountains.
Percussive water impact large crash or slam of water as in huge wave hitting an object. Bigger and sharper than a SPLASH
Water splashing against something like against the side of a boat or canoe or a dock. Also meant for very small waves lapping on shore like gentle very small waves at night.
Water sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Movement in water but not underwater (see WATER-UNDERWATER) Drags through water swimming movements sweeps of objects through water. Thrashing in water.
Household plumbing like a sink running a toilet flush the shower running. DRAIN has its own subcategory.
Pouring water onto ground into a cup. Overlap with FOOD & DRINK-POUR.
Water splashes of all kind a person diving into a pool a whale cresting throwing a rock into water.
Big sprays of a wave crash to a hose spray.
Very wet hisses radiators saunas. Hot wet steam.
The general roar of the ocean not the sport. Constant ocean roaring waves.
Very big splashy water. Whirlpools big rapids on a river. Dangerous water.
Specifically underwater sounds flows current and movement Swimming underwater. See also AMBIENCE-UNDERWATER and WATER-MOVEMENT as well.
Waterfalls from small cascading ones to Niagara falls.
For general water waves distinct waves. General ocean roar to go in WATER-SURF and tiny waves to WATER-LAPPING. See also NATURAL DISASTERS-TSUNAMI.
Knights armor movement hits. Shields chainmail armor leather armor with buckles.
Arrow slides arrow impacts. An arrow by would likely be a SWOOSHES-SWISH. See also WEAPONS-BOW.
Specifically a weaponized ax. Possible overlap with WEAPONS-SWORD or even WEAPON-POLEARM.
Blunt weapons like maces and flails but also nightsticks.
Bows and Crossbows articulation twangs bolts being loaded. Bow releases. See also WEAPON-ARROW.
Smaller knives WEAPONS-SWORDS has it own subcategory here.
Weapon sounds not fitting another category in this list.
A long spear or long poled weapon with sharp components at the end like a harpoon.
A siege weapon is a device designed to break or circumvent fortifications catapults trebuchets. Ancient artillery.
Swords clashing parrying. Sword rings and hits. STABS would go to GORE category. Sword draws shings.
Whip cracks. Make Indiana Jones proud.
Hail on various surfaces.
Weather sounds not fitting another category in this list. See also NATURAL DISASTER category.
Composite storms wind rain thunder where elements aren't isolated. Mixed storm composite recordings. Also see WIND-TURBULENT for wind only elements and NATURAL-DISASTER.
Thunder and lightning dry wet distant rumbles to sharp thunder cracks. Combination RAIN & THUNDER may best go into WEATHER-STORM
Human whistles wolf-whistles. See also CARTOON-WHISTLES.
Train whistles referee whistles tea pot whistles etc any whistle not made by a human.
Whistle sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Designed or manufactured or artificial winds wind machines synth winds.
Base and general wind category.
For more singular gusts or buffets accents to cut into other winds. Usually shorter. A constant buffeting wind better off in the GENERAL or TURBULENT category.
Interior wind through window or crack in door. Wind through chimney or vent pipe but see also WIND-TONAL for overtly tonal winds and whistles.
Wind howls wind in wires wind whistles overtly tonal winds.
Extremely violent wind big buffeting huge storm winds. Possible overlap with NATURAL DISASTER-TORNADO or WEATHER-STORM.
Wind through foliage vegetation grass trees leaves plants.
Window curtains blinds shutters. So a window covering not the window itself.
Window latches cranks locks. Window hardware.
Knocking on various window surfaces knocking on GLASS.
Metal framed windows open and close.
Window sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Plastic framed windows plexiglass windows. Vinyl framed windows open and close.
Wood framed windows open and close.
Bird wing flaps hummingbirds pigeon wings. Group or single.
Dragon wings fairy wings any manner of creature and monster wings.
Fly and bee wings dragonflies moths butterflies. Any insect wings. Some overlap and confusion with ANIMALS-INECTS likely.
Wing sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Wood breaks shatters cracks crunches. Any destruction of wood that breaks the piece.
Group wood crashes and debris and pieces falling as in after an explosion.
Wood stress especially creaks. But also scrapes squeaks.
Handling a wooden object picking up setting down etc. Some overlap or confusion with WOOD-MOVEMENT.
Singular wood hits drops impacts board hits. Group wood destruction goes into CRASH. Resonant wood bonks or hits. Hitting your head on a doorframe.
Wood sounds not fitting another category in this list.
Wood movement including rattles drag slides rolls. Group pieces of wood moving together.
Tonal bowed wood sounds ringing wood.
